test.name: Detect an implausible lifespan over 120 years (Fundamental violation)
test.description: User asks to check Patrick. The tool returns hasAgeRangeGreaterThan120 — the gap between his earliest possible birth year and latest possible death year exceeds 120 years. The skill should report this as Critical and recognize it as a strong signal of either a wrong vital date or two distinct individuals merged into one profile. Tests the severity classification rubric dimension (Fundamental → error / Critical).

input.user_message: Run a warnings check on Patrick Flynn.

judge_context: Should report the hasAgeRangeGreaterThan120 warning with Critical severity
Should recommend verifying both vital dates against source documents — either could be the wrong one
Should mention the identity-confusion possibility: clustering between very old recorded death and very early recorded birth is one of the classic two-people-merged signatures
Should NOT label this as a minor 'unusually long life' note (Low severity); the tool's >120 threshold is the Fundamental tier, not the Low/Medium tier of biological implausibility
